ops.title: Quarantine dual-use security content in isolated agents to avoid Fable downgrade
ops.body:
Fable's dual-use safety screening can downgrade a session to opus when its context carries flagged security material — exploit code, vuln PoCs, malware analysis, offensive tooling. A downgraded **root orchestrator** loses the fable lane (orchestration, synthesis, prose) for the rest of the session.

**The rule** (canonical: `plugin/guides/md/claude-rules.md`, renders into CLAUDE.md fleet-wide via each repo's daily guides cron): such material never sits in the root agent's context. An isolated subagent, teammate, or workflow lane reads and writes the payloads and returns conclusions and pointers — a downgrade then costs one delegate, not the session. The codex security lanes already have this shape (they run outside Claude entirely); Claude-side sensitive implementation runs as a fable subagent, not inline. Routing is unchanged: the Models table still picks who does the work; this rule fixes where the content sits.

Retells: the codex SKILL security bullet (codex plugin 0.19.1) and repo-bootstrap base-conventions (0.56.0); codex-wrapper carries no security retell so it is unchanged. Landed in cc-skills 4a759e9317ca.
ops.when: when designing agent/workflow structures for security material, or implementing dual-use content handling to avoid session downgrade
